user_query:
I want to establish a lightweight self-improvement log for our repo based on a recent session. Please do the following:

1) Read inputs under input/:
- input/session_log.jsonl (a short transcript with an install failure, a correction, and a feature wish)
- input/project_context.md (brief context about the project)
- input/config.yaml (provides the ID date and numbering guidance)

2) Create a .learnings/ directory under output/ and append exactly one new entry to each file below using a structured format. Use the ID date from input/config.yaml and a three-digit ID number for each entry. The three entries should reference the same incidents from the transcript:
- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md → one error entry for the npm vs pnpm install failure. Include:
  - Header: "## [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX] <short_name>"
  - Fields: "**Logged**: <ISO-8601 timestamp>", "**Priority**: high", "**Status**: pending", "**Area**: infra | config"
  - Sections: "### Summary" (brief), "### Error" (a fenced code block containing the actual error text mentioning pnpm-lock.yaml and npm install), "### Context" (bullet points: command attempted, inputs/parameters, environment if relevant), "### Suggested Fix" (e.g., use pnpm install), "### Metadata" (include Reproducible yes/no and Related Files if any)

- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md → one learning entry derived from the user correction that we use pnpm (not npm). Include:
  - Header: "## [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] best_practice" or "correction" (pick the most appropriate)
  - Fields: "**Logged**: <ISO-8601 timestamp>", "**Priority**: medium", "**Status**: pending (then update to promoted in step 4)", "**Area**: infra | config"
  - Sections: "### Summary" (one line), "### Details" (what was wrong and what is correct), "### Suggested Action" (clear next step), "### Metadata" (Source: conversation, Tags, and a See Also field that links to the related ERR ID)

- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md → one feature request based on the wish to automatically condense long error logs into a concise summary. Include:
  - Header: "## [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX] log_summarization"
  - Fields: "**Logged**: <ISO-8601 timestamp>", "**Priority**: medium", "**Status**: pending", "**Area**: docs | infra"
  - Sections: "### Requested Capability" (describe what the user wanted), "### User Context" (why they need it), "### Complexity Estimate" (simple | medium | complex), "### Suggested Implementation" (how it could work), "### Metadata" (Frequency and Related Features if any)

3) Use ID format TYPE-YYYYMMDD-XXX where YYYYMMDD is the id_date from input/config.yaml and XXX is a three-digit sequence (e.g., 001, 002, 003). Each of the three entries must have a unique ID with that date.

4) Promote the pnpm-related learning to a concise, project-wide rule in output/CLAUDE.md, such as under a "Build & Dependencies" section with a bullet on using pnpm. Then update the corresponding learning entry in output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md to set "**Status**: promoted" and add a "Promoted: CLAUDE.md" note in the learning entry.

5) Keep everything in English, use only relative workspace paths, and do not write outside output/. Ensure the entries are specific and actionable (not vague), and link the error and learning via See Also.

Deliverables to create:
- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md (with one new ERR entry)
- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md (with one new LRN entry that is promoted and links to the ERR)
- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md (with one new FEAT entry)
- output/CLAUDE.md (contains the distilled pnpm rule)
